name: Fried Oysters Wrapped in Bacon
recipeIngredient: 2 EACH EGGS (BEATEN IN A SMALL BOWL)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 1 quart saucepan, on a medium flame, poach the oysters in oyster liquor with bay leaf and Worcestershire sauce for about 2 minutes, until the edges of the oysters curl. Remove the oysters from the liquor and set aside. Discard the liquor.
2. Cut bacon strips into thirds. Wrap each oyster individually with a strip of bacon and secure with a toothpick.
3. In a shallow dish, coat each oyster in flour, shaking off any excess.
4. In another shallow dish, beat the eggs.
5. Dip each oyster in the egg mixture, then return to the dish with flour and roll to coat evenly.
6. In a third shallow dish, sprinkle bread crumbs over the oysters.
7. Heat oil in a 9 inch skillet until smoking.
8. Reduce heat so the oil just begins to smoke gently.
9. Carefully place each bacon-wrapped oyster into the hot oil and fry for 5 minutes, or until golden brown.
10. Using tongs, remove the fried oysters from the oil and drain on a paper towel-lined plate.
11. Serve immediately.
Yields 12 oysters.
